title: Technical Project Coordinator
description:
We are seeking a highly organized and detail-oriented Technical Project Coordinator to join our growing team.

Responsibilities include:

• Assisting with the coordination of custom software, CRM, automation, and website projects.
• Maintaining project plans, priorities, timelines, and next steps across multiple active client projects.
• Tracking project progress and following up with tea ---------- mbers regarding open items, blockers, and deadlines.
• Preparing project status updates and summaries for management.
• Identifying projects that are falling behind schedule or require management attention.
• Attending internal and client meetings and documenting requirements, decisions, action items, assumptions, open questions, and next steps.
• Assisting with the preparation of project briefs, specifications, and Jira tasks based on client meetings, emails, and discussions.
• Converting meeting notes, emails, and client requests into organized project documentation, requirements summaries, and Jira tasks.
• Identifying missing requirements, open questions, and potential project risks.
• Assisting with light review of completed work against documented requirements when needed.
• Helping maintain project documentation, specifications, and implementation plans.
• Supporting communication and coordination between Operations, Engineering, and client-facing staff.
• Helping ensure that no client project falls through the cracks as the company continues to grow.

The ideal candidate will be highly organized, proactive, detail-oriented, and comfortable managing many moving pieces simultaneously. Strong written and spoken English communication skills are required. Experience working with software development, website projects, CRM systems, Jira, project management tools, or technical teams is preferred but not required.

This role is ideal for someone who enjoys creating structure, keeping projects organized, tracking follow-up items, documenting requirements, and helping teams stay aligned and on schedule.

This is a full-time position. Because this role works closely with tea ---------- mbers and clients in the United States, candidates must be willing and able to work primarily during United States Eastern Time business hours.

Immediately upon hiring, we honor most Filipino holidays except for All Saints and All Souls Day. After your 6-month probationary period, you will also qualify for vacation leaves, sick leaves, and 13th month.

Founded in 2006, Organize My People is a technology implementation and consulting firm focused on helping organizations utilize CRM systems, automation tools, custom software, and websites to improve their operations. Our clients include nonprofit organizations, educational institutions, event organizations, faith-based institutions, and other businesses throughout the United States, Canada, and Australia.

title: PHP / Laravel Software Developer with API Experience
description:
We are seeking a talented and experienced individual to join our growing team as a PHP / Laravel Software Developer with strong API integration experience. Responsibilities include:

• Building PHP-based web platforms for our clients which interface with either MySQL or Microsoft SQL databases.
• Updating existing PHP-based web platforms for our clients with new functionality, including platforms developed in native PHP, Laravel and Nuxt.js.
• Connecting PHP-based platforms with third party tools via their APIs.
• Building and maintaining scheduled data sync tools, webhook listeners, API polling jobs and integration scripts.
• Working with REST APIs, JSON, OAuth, API keys, pagination, rate limits, error handling and logging.
• Technical support for bugs/errors identified in existing client PHP-based web platforms and API integrations.

The ideal candidate will have experience with PHP development, Laravel, third-party API integrations, and either MySQL or Microsoft SQL databases. The ideal candidate will also have an attention to detail, the ability to complete tasks on time, a stable internet connection, and strong written English communication skills.

Full time, part time or contract based will all be considered based on your preferences.  Immediately upon hiring, we honor most Filipino holidays except for All Saints and All Souls Day. After your 6-month probationary period, you will also qualify for vacation leaves, sick leaves, and 13th month.

Founded in 2006, Organize My People is a strategy consulting, technology implementation and software development firm focused on helping organizations utilize technology to drive their missions. Clients include nonprofit organizations, event production companies, law firms, faith-based institutions, property management companies, healthcare offices, scientific research organizations, personal blogs and real estate investment companies. title: Founding Systems Integrator — GoHighLevel, AI Automation & No-Code/Low-Code Systems
description:
# PAY

Starting monthly compensation in PHP, based on experience, proof of work, expected weekly hours, and fit.

We are cost-conscious because we are still early-stage, but we will pay fairly for someone who can actually solve the right problems.

This may start part-time or ramp up over time depending on workload, performance, and mutual fit.


# ROLE TYPE

Remote contractor role. Long-term opportunity for the right person.


# COMPANY

Atlas Cybertech

---

# ABOUT THE COMPANY

Atlas Cybertech is a Canadian AI automation and systems company building internal platforms, CRM infrastructure, automation workflows, and operational systems.

We are early-stage and moving quickly toward revenue. That means we need to get our baseline systems live fast, without creating a fragile mess that breaks later.

Good-enough, working systems are better than perfect systems that never launch. But “good enough” still needs to be clear, testable, and maintainable.

We are not looking for AI hype, random tool-stacking, or “automate everything” thinking.

We are looking for someone who can help us build practical MVP systems quickly, especially around GoHighLevel, AI-assisted workflows, no-code/low-code tools, automations, integrations, and internal operations.

---

# ROLE SUMMARY

This is a *GoHighLevel-heavy role.*

GoHighLevel is one of the most important platforms in our business, so you must have real hands-on GHL experience.

This role is not GHL-only, but strong GHL ability is central.

You will help build and improve Atlas Cybertech’s internal systems across:

- GoHighLevel
- CRM workflows
- No-code/low-code tools
- Automations
- Forms and intake flows
- System of Record structures
- Internal portals or dashboards
- AI-assisted documentation and SOPs
- Light AI-assisted workflows
- API/webhook-based integrations where needed

We are especially interested in someone who is personally excited about AI and actively experiments with AI tools, AI coding assistants, agentic workflows, automation tools, and new ways to use AI to build faster and work smarter.

You do not need to be a full-stack software developer or AI engineer.

You do need to be technically sharp, AI-curious, systems-minded, and capable of figuring things out.

---

# JOB MISSION

Your mission is to help get Atlas Cybertech’s internal operating stack online quickly, then improve it over time.

The first priority is helping us get our core MVP systems configured, connected, tested, documented, and usable.

The goal is to create systems that:

- Reduce manual work
- Help us move toward revenue faster
- Keep data reasonably clean
- Avoid silent failures
- Make workflows easier to understand
- Make automations easier to troubleshoot
- Support future client delivery
- Can be documented, maintained, and handed off later


This role is ideal for someone who naturally asks:

- How do we get this live quickly without making a mess?
- Where is the bottleneck?
- What data should live where?
- What happens if this automation fails?
- What is the simplest working version?
- How can AI help us build, document, or troubleshoot faster?
- How can another person maintain this later?

---

# WHAT YOU'LL WORK ON

You may help with:

- Building and improving GoHighLevel workflows, pipelines, tags, custom fields, calendars, forms, automations, snapshots, triggers, and integrations.

- Setting up and troubleshooting GHL sub-accounts, workflow logic, CRM stages, and client-style account structures.

- Helping structure data in systems like SmartSuite, Airtable, or similar tools.

- Connecting forms, CRM, automations, dashboards, portals, and documentation workflows.

- Building no-code/low-code automations using tools like Make, Zapier, n8n, or similar platforms.

- Troubleshooting broken workflows, triggers, integrations, data-sync issues, and automation failures.

- Creating AI-assisted SOPs, workflow maps, field maps, process notes, Loom walkthroughs, and system documentation.

- Helping build internal systems for hiring, onboarding, lead handling, client intake, follow-up, and operations.

- Supporting AI-assisted workflows where AI helps the process but does not become the source of truth.

- Using AI-assisted light coding where appropriate for small scripts, API calls, webhook troubleshooting, data transformations, or internal workflow helpers.

- Improving system reliability, error visibility, speed of execution, and handoff readiness.

You do not need to be a professional writer, but you do need to care about leaving clear notes and using AI tools to make documentation faster and easier.

---

# IMPORTANT ARCHITECTURE PRINCIPLE

We separate tools by role.

- The System of Record is the main source of truth for important business data.
- GoHighLevel is the CRM and workflow layer.
- Forms are for intake.
- Portals and dashboards are for viewing or interacting with data.
- Automation tools move data and trigger workflows.
- AI tools can assist, but they do not own the process.

GoHighLevel is very important, but it is not automatically the System of Record for everything.

If this distinction makes sense to you, this role may be a strong fit.

---

# REQUIRED EXPERIENCE

You should have real experience with:

- GoHighLevel workflows, pipelines, tags, custom fields, triggers, calendars, forms, and automations.

- GHL troubleshooting and workflow behavior.

- CRM setup, pipeline logic, and automation logic.

- No-code/low-code automation tools such as Make, Zapier, n8n, or similar.

- Basic API/webhook concepts.

- Data fields, records, statuses, permissions, and workflow stages.

- Troubleshooting broken automations or integrations.

- Clear documentation, SOPs, workflow maps, or process notes.

- Explaining technical decisions in plain English.

- Using AI tools to improve speed, research, documentation, troubleshooting, or technical implementation.


You should also have strong personal interest in AI.

Ideally, you already spend some of your own time testing, researching, or experimenting with tools like ChatGPT, Claude, Gemini, DeepSeek, Codex-style tools, AI coding assistants, AI agents, automation agents, or other AI tools.

You do not need to be a full-stack software developer or AI engineer.

You do need to be technically sharp enough to understand workflows, data movement, integration logic, AI-assisted workflows, prompting, failure points, and system handoff.

---

# HIGH-PRIORITY EXPERIENCE

These are especially valuable:

- GoHighLevel snapshots
- GHL SaaS mode
- GHL agency/sub-account setup
- GHL AI features or AI Employee-style workflows
- Client account cloning or snapshot deployment
- Twilio or SMS/calling workflows
- A2P / SMS approval workflows
- AI appointment-setting bots
- AI voice/SMS/chat workflows
- Advanced AI tools, AI coding tools, agentic tools, or automation agents

The more experience you have with GHL and AI-assisted systems, the better.

---

# STRONG BONUS EXPERIENCE

These are not all required, but they are useful:

- SmartSuite
- Airtable
- Fillout
- Softr
- Noloco
- Bubble / Glide / Loveable
- Google Workspace / Google Drive
- Microsoft 365
- Meta / Facebook Ads lead flow
- Lead forms, UTM tracking, pipeline attribution, or conversion tracking
- KPI dashboards or funnel reporting
- Lightweight JavaScript, Python, JSON, or API troubleshooting
- Experience supporting agencies, consultants, founder-led companies, or service businesses

You do not need to be a media buyer, but paid ads platform literacy is a plus because many systems will connect to lead generation workflows.

---

# THIS ROLE IS *NOT* FOR YOU IF

Please do not apply if:

- You are mainly a VA with light tool experience.
- You only know basic GHL admin tasks.
- You only build funnels and landing pages.
- You only know simple Zapier automations.
- You describe yourself mainly as a prompt engineer.
- You want a CTO, executive, or management title.
- You prefer custom code before checking simpler options.
- You add new tools before understanding the current system.
- You automate unstable workflows without mapping them first.
- You do not like documenting your work.
- You give vague answers like “I can automate everything.”
- You rely on AI-generated answers without real examples.
- You are not personally interested in learning and testing new AI tools.

This role is not for beginners.

---

# WORKING STYLE

This role is a good fit if you are:

- Strong with GoHighLevel.
- Highly interested in AI tools and automation.
- Practical and MVP-minded.
- Systems-minded.
- Strong at troubleshooting.
- Fast, but not reckless.
- Comfortable figuring things out independently.
- Clear in written and spoken English.
- Comfortable working directly with a founder.
- Willing to use AI to speed up documentation and technical work.
- Able to explain tradeoffs clearly.
- Willing to challenge weak ideas respectfully.
- Focused on building systems that other people can maintain later.

We want speed, but not reckless speed.

Good-enough, working systems are better than perfect systems that never launch. But “good enough” still needs to be clear, testable, and maintainable.

---

# AUTHORITY BOUNDARIES

You will be expected to recommend, build, test, troubleshoot, and document systems.

However:

- You do not choose new core platforms without approval.
- You do not turn GHL into the System of Record by default.
- You do not add tools without a clear reason.
- You do not build custom-code dependencies unless clearly justified and approved.
- You do not redesign the core architecture without discussion.

The founder makes final architecture and platform decisions.

Your role is to recommend, execute, document, and improve.

---

# HIRING PROCESS

The hiring process is designed to avoid long live interviews.

Shortlisted applicants may complete:

- A short recorded screening
- A more detailed scenario-based assessment
- One paid practical task if selected as a finalist
- A short 10-15 minute founder call as the final step

The paid practical task is only for finalists. We do not ask many candidates to do unpaid project work.

---

# HOW TO APPLY

At the top of your application, write this exact phrase:
*Atlas Systems*
Then answer the questions below.

Please answer all screening questions directly in your application. Applications that do not answer the questions will not be considered.

Generic AI-written answers will be rejected. Use specific examples from systems you have personally built, fixed, documented, improved, or tested.

---

# APPLICATION QUESTIONS

1. Evidence of Ability

In 3-5 bullet points, describe the strongest evidence of your ability for this role.

Focus on difficult systems, GoHighLevel builds, automations, workflow problems, AI-assisted builds, or technical issues you personally solved.


2. Proof of Work

Share any relevant proof of work.

Examples:

- Loom videos
- Screenshots
- Diagrams
- SOPs
- Documentation
- Portfolio links
- Workflow maps
- Case studies

Do not share confidential client data.


3. Tool and Skill Experience

List the main tools or tool categories you have real experience with.

For each category, mention the specific tools, what you personally used them for, and your approximate skill level from 1-10.

Categories:

- GoHighLevel / CRM systems: 
Workflows, pipelines, snapshots, SaaS mode, sub-accounts, automations, troubleshooting

- Automation tools: 
Make, Zapier, n8n, Pabbly, Relay, or similar

- No-code/low-code tools: 
Airtable, SmartSuite, Fillout, Softr, Noloco, Bubble, Glide, or similar

- AI tools / AI coding / agentic tools: 
ChatGPT, Claude, Codex-style tools, Gemini, DeepSeek, AI agents, AI coding tools, automation agents, or similar

- API / webhook / data integration work: 
Webhooks, JSON, API calls, data transformations, scripts

- Paid ads / lead-flow systems: 
Meta Ads, lead forms, tracking, attribution, conversion events, UTM/source tracking

- SMS/calling systems: 
Twilio, A2P, calling/SMS workflows, notification systems

- Business ops tools: 
Google Workspace, Microsoft 365, ClickUp, Notion, Slack, Google Sheets


You are not expected to know every tool listed above. 

These are examples to help you explain your experience clearly. 
GoHighLevel experience is the most important. 

Strong AI interest and automation ability are also very important. 
The more relevant tools and systems you know, the better.


4. Availability and Starting Compensation 

Please provide the following:

- Desired starting monthly compensation in PHP for the first 1-3 months while onboarding, learning our systems, and ramping up. 

- Number of hours per week this monthly amount assumes. 

- Maximum hours per week you are available to work consistently. 

- Target long-term monthly compensation in PHP after the first 1-3 months, assuming strong performance and good fit. 

- Usual work window in Philippine time, such as morning, afternoon, evening, late night, overnight, or flexible.

- Earliest available start date. 

---

# FINAL NOTE

We are cost-conscious because we are still early-stage, but we will pay fairly for someone who can actually solve the right problems.

We are looking for the best mix of capability, reliability, speed, judgment, AI interest, GHL ability, and reasonable cost.

If you enjoy building systems, solving technical puzzles, experimenting with AI tools, improving workflows, and making operations run better, this role may be a strong fit.

title: Virtual Assistant - Sales Support & Data Entry
description:
JOB OVERVIEW
About GraphicLab
GraphicLab is a fast-paced promotional products company based in California. We handle big clients, big projects, and in-house screen printing and embroidery. You will act as the direct support line for our Sales Team, ensuring that our internal systems run flawlessly so our sales team can focus on client relationships.

The Role
We are seeking a highly reliable and detail-oriented Virtual Assistant to manage the critical backend data of our sales process, working directly with a senior sales supervisor. You will be responsible for taking quotes and emailed instructions from our California team and ensuring it is accurately entered into our CRM. You will be explicitly trained on our standards and procedures ("the GraphicLab way") by our lead VA.

In addition, during slower periods you will support light marketing execution, creating email and social media outreach campaigns using CRM/customer data to help us re-engage past customers and generate new leads.

KEY RESPONSIBILITIES
Data Entry & CRM Management: Accurately enter new jobs and orders into our CRM system (CommonSKU). 

Daily Follow-Up & Calendar Management: Proactively stay on top of calendar, daily communications, and follow-ups.

Quoting Support: Assist in building and organizing quotes for new jobs based on provided pricing parameters.

Order Tracking: Monitor the status of jobs by checking in with production schedules and external vendors.

Logistics: Compile and organize shipping/tracking information to be sent to clients.

Communication: Maintain clear, daily communication with the California-based Sales Team to provide end-of-shift progress reports.


Email + Social Campaign Support (Slow-Season Outreach):

Use CRM data to pull lists/segments (past customers, warm leads, target industries) for outreach.

Build and send email campaigns (subject lines, copy, basic layout) to re-engage customers and drive quote requests.

Create simple social media content (IG + LinkedIn) using provided brand direction: captions + basic graphics (Canva-style).

Schedule posts and coordinate a simple content calendar.

Track and report results (what was sent/posted, opens/clicks/replies, what to improve next round).

REQUIREMENTS
Industry Experience: Knowledge of the Beverage Industry is highly important. Experience in the Promotional Products / Apparel industry is ideal. Having experience in both is considered a massive plus.

Communication Style (Crucial): You must be a loud, clear, and confident communicator; therefore, candidates who are soft-spoken or meek will not be a good fit.

Shift Requirement: You must be available to work a strict 40-hour week on the US Pacific Standard Time (PST) schedule to entirely overlap with our California office. Note: Hours are strictly capped at 40 per week and cannot be exceeded unless requested in writing.

lawless English (Written and Spoken): You must be able to write clear, professional emails and CRM communications. You will attend virtual meetings with our internal team and occasionally with our customers.

Attention to Detail: You must be highly diligent; a single typo in a SKU number or choosing the wrong color can cost the company money. Accuracy is paramount.

Tech-Savvy: Familiarity with CRM systems is a plus, but a strong general ability to learn new software quickly is required (MS Office, Google Workspace, Adobe, etc.).

Marketing Execution Skills: You must be comfortable using CRM data to support outbound email + social campaigns, writing clear outreach copy, and creating simple on-brand visuals (Canva or similar).

HOW TO APPLY
Submit your resume.

Include a brief cover letter highlighting your experience in the Beverage and/or Promotional Products industries, your experience with data entry/CRM management, and any marketing support background.

A 1 to 2-Minute Camera Loom Video: Please ensure your speaking voice is projected loudly and clearly in this video. Introduce yourself, briefly mention your CRM experience, your background working as a VA on the night shift (PST), and answer these scenarios:

If a vendor emails you to say a shipment of 500 shirts is delayed by 3 days, what are the exact steps you take to update our system and notify our California sales team?

During a slow week, you’re asked to re-engage past customers. What CRM info would you pull, and what would your first email campaign look like (who it targets + subject line + goal)?

title: Junior Bookkeeper – Ironbooks
description:
**Location:** Philippines | **Type:** Full-time Remote

---

## The Vision

We're building the AI-powered bookkeeping service for trades contractors. And we're doing it differently.

Most bookkeeping firms are dinosaurs—slow, expensive, offshore labor treated as a cost center. We're proving the opposite: that the best bookkeepers aren't in expensive US metros. They're smart, hungry, high-D/high-C operators who want to build something real.

**You're not joining a bookkeeping team. You're joining the proof of concept that AI + offshore talent + ruthless execution = the future of accounting operations.**

Every client we onboard. Every book you clean up. Every report you own. That's market validation that traditional accounting firms are about to get disrupted.

---

## What You're Actually Building

This role is ground zero for something bigger:

- **You're the backbone of Ironbooks' operations.** Every client win flows through the work you do. You're not hidden in the back office—your work directly drives revenue, client retention, and company reputation.

- **You're proving a thesis.** We're showing that a Filipino bookkeeper + Claude AI + clear processes = better, faster, cheaper than traditional US-based accounting. That's not a side hustle. That's category-defining.

- **You're scaling a system, not just doing work.** Right now, you're learning. In 6 months, you're training others. In 12 months, you're leading a team and working with our CEO on process improvement. This grows with you.

- **You're part of the Painter Growth ecosystem.** Ironbooks isn't standalone—it's the operational proof point for Painter Growth's AI positioning across the trades. Your work is literally the case study we use to sell coaching and other services. High visibility. Real leverage.

---

## Why This Matters (For You)

**If you're the type who wants more than a job:**

- You'll see direct ROI on your effort. Ship a clean book ? client signs for annual contract ? you see the impact immediately.
- You're not a cost center. You're a revenue driver. That matters differently when you know it.
- You're learning a category-defining skill set: bookkeeping + AI automation + operational systems. That's not replaceable. That's your edge.
- There's a real path here. We're scaling. The people who prove themselves in the first 6 months aren't stuck in junior roles—they move into leadership, specialist tracks, or new ventures as we expand.
- You're working with founders who care about outcomes and speed, not politics. That changes everything about how work feels.

---

## The Reality

We're lean. We move fast. We don't tolerate slow thinking or excuses. But we also:

- Celebrate wins loudly (and mean it)
- Pay attention to people who deliver
- Build in public (you'll see how your work impacts the market)
- Give autonomy to people who earn it
- Actually promote from within

---

## What We Need

**Must-haves:**
- 2+ years of bookkeeping or accounting experience
- Fluent English (writing and speaking)
- Comfortable with QuickBooks Online
- High D energy: you want to win, you move fast, you own your output
- High C discipline: you catch errors, you care about quality, you want to build systems that work
- Openness to AI tools—this is non-negotiable. You'll use Claude, ChatGPT, and automation to multiply your productivity
- Reliable internet and a workspace where you can focus

**Nice-to-haves:**
- Experience with AI tools (you already know how to use them)
- Previous remote work or offshore experience
- Familiarity with Zapier or integration platforms
- Trades or contractor business background

---

## The First 90 Days

- **Month 1:** You're learning our systems, processes, and standards. You're doing deep work on client cleanups and learning to work alongside our AI tools.
- **Month 2:** You're owning client relationships independently. You're proofing your own work, shipping reports, and starting to see patterns in what works.
- **Month 3:** You're refining our processes. You're identifying bottlenecks. You're having ideas about how we do things better. We're listening.

By the end of 90 days, we know if you're the one who grows with us.

---

## Compensation & Benefits

- **Salary:** $7/hr with opportunities for bonuses and raises quickly if demonstrating high quality and great work ethic
- **Hours:** 8 hours/day, PST-aligned (flexible within reason)
- **Tools:** All software, licenses, and AI tools provided
- **Growth trajectory:** Clear progression to senior bookkeeper, operations specialist, or leadership roles
- **Equity conversation:** After 6 months of proving yourself, we talk about how you can own a piece of this upside

---

## Send Us

1. Your resume (focused on results and ROI, not just tasks)
2. 2–3 bookkeeping examples you're genuinely proud of
3. A short note: *Why this role? What does "building something" mean to you? Give us the real answer.*

---

**This isn't a generic bookkeeping job.** We're looking for someone who sees the bigger picture and wants to be part of proving that offshore + AI + execution = the future of accounting.

If that's you, let's talk.

title: Executive Assistant
description:
Executive Assistant
Fully Remote, Part-Time

ABOUT YOU
You have a refining nature that is always looking for how to make things a little bit better. You notice the small things like a repeating conversation, a habit people fall into, or an unnoticed shortcut. You adapt or suggest changes to others, so those patterns become opportunities for growth.

You care about doing things intentionally. You think things through, break them down clearly, notice details others miss, and bring a sense of order to what you take on.

You’re naturally curious... the kind of person who wants to understand how things work and why something is the way it is. You look for context, ask clarifying questions, and enjoy figuring things out until the “why” becomes clear.

You move through life with a deep belief that everything is for your good. You don’t see challenges as setbacks, you see them as a puzzle to solve, a lesson to learn, or an opportunity for growth. At your core, you’re a glass-half-full person.

ABOUT US
We’re a technical services business currently supporting Cisco systems and moving into AI-based solutions. As a tiny company, it will be just the owner and you. This means communication is direct, decisions are fast, and your work has a real impact on the business & its growth.

title: Technical Support Specialist
description:
Solve the Hard Stuff. Keep Customers Moving. 

JOB SUMMARY 

Cybernautic's customers need fast, reliable technical support from someone who actually knows what they're doing. That's where you come in.  

As a Technical Support Specialist, you'll handle the majority of website-related support tickets — HTML, CSS, JavaScript, PHP, CMS updates, bug fixes, DNS records, and email hosting — so our development team stays focused on building great things. You'll be the first line of defense, and you'll resolve most issues without needing to escalate. 

This is a technically sharp, code-capable support role for someone who loves solving problems, communicates clearly, and takes ownership from first contact to resolution. You'll work across our in-house CMS (Editor), Shopify, Cloudflare, GoDaddy, Rackspace and OpenSRS Email, FreshDesk, and more — and you'll use AI tools to troubleshoot faster, write better, and work smarter. 

This role is based in the Philippines and requires availability during U.S. business hours. You'll work closely with our U.S.-based Client Services Specialist, communicate directly with clients by email and occasionally by phone or video, and hold yourself to a high standard — because our customers do. 

If you have real website development experience, strong English communication skills, and the instincts to investigate problems thoroughly before asking for help — this role was built for you. 

WHAT YOU'LL DO 

Website & CMS Support 
* Troubleshoot, resolve, and directly implement fixes for website support tickets across front-end and back-end issues 
* Create, modify, and troubleshoot CMS content structures to support client requests 
* Provide CMS training and guidance to website administrators as needed 
* Use appropriate technical tools and resources to investigate issues and identify root causes 
* Test all changes before closing a ticket — browser compatibility, mobile responsiveness, form functionality, and unintended side effects 
* Document findings, steps taken, files changed, and resolutions clearly within every ticket 
* Protect website stability by following established development, testing, backup, and change-management practices before making updates to live client websites 
* Investigate and resolve the majority of website issues independently, escalating to the development team only when necessary and always with clear documentation 
*  Keep the Client Services Specialist informed of ticket status, technical findings, and escalation needs 

DNS & Email Hosting Support 
* Assist clients with DNS record configuration in GoDaddy and Cloudflare 
* Troubleshoot and resolve SSL certificate issues, including expired certificates, mixed content warnings, and domain verification problems 
* Support clients using Rackspace Email and assist with migrating accounts to OpenSRS, including DNS setup, mailbox migration, and client coordination 
* Help clients configure Rackspace and OpenSRS email accounts into leading email applications including Outlook, Gmail, and mobile mail clients 
* Identify and escalate systemic email delivery or routing issues to vendors as needed 

Shopify Support 
* Troubleshoot and resolve Shopify storefront issues including theme layout problems, navigation, page content, section and block configuration, and checkout display issues 
* Assist clients with connecting custom domains to Shopify, including DNS configuration, SSL verification, and subdomain and redirect setup 
* Help clients with basic Shopify admin tasks including product setup, collections, metafields, and page content management 
* Escalate complex Shopify development requests — such as custom development, advanced Liquid logic, or API integrations — to a lead developer with full documentation 

Customer & Ticket Management 
* Interact with clients to provide timely support and information across all assigned channels 
* Manage the full ticket lifecycle: opening, assigning, monitoring, escalating, and resolving support tickets 
* Troubleshoot and resolve general account and access issues, including password resets, account lockouts, and client inquiries not handled by a dedicated support section 
* Maintain accurate records of client interactions, including details of inquiries, complaints, and actions taken 
* Escalate unresolved or complex issues to the appropriate team member or vendor partner with thorough documentation 
* Fulfill all service level standards for response time and quality 

Knowledge Management 
* Develop and maintain a library of support articles, how-to guides, and best practices  
* Flag outdated or inaccurate articles and update them to reflect current products and procedures 
* Document troubleshooting patterns and contribute new content that reduces repeat ticket volume 

AI-Assisted Workflows 
* Incorporate AI tools into daily support workflows to improve response quality, efficiency, and consistency 
* Use AI to assist with code troubleshooting, research, documentation, and client communications — always verifying AI-generated output before applying it to live systems 
* Stay current on emerging AI tools and recommend new applications that benefit the support team 

WHAT WE'RE LOOKING FOR 

You'll need: 
* Practical experience troubleshooting and updating websites using HTML, CSS, JavaScript, and PHP, including CMS-driven sites — content updates, layout fixes, template edits, and moderately complex code changes 
* Strong understanding of website hosting, domains, and DNS, including the ability to create, update, troubleshoot, and migrate records such as A, CNAME, MX, TXT, SPF, DKIM, and DMARC 
* Experience troubleshooting email setup and delivery issues, including mailbox configuration, configuring email into client applications, DNS-related email problems, and basic deliverability issues 
* Familiarity with FTP/SFTP, hosting control panels, CMS admin tools, browser developer tools, and basic server resources for investigating and resolving issues 
* Comfortable using AI tools to assist with technical troubleshooting, code review, research, documentation, and rewriting client replies — with good judgment about verifying recommendations before applying them 
* Ability to investigate technical problems independently, review code and configurations thoroughly, document findings clearly, and balance speed with accuracy on live systems 
* Strong English communication skills — written and verbal — including clear, professional client-facing responses and the ability to join client calls when needed 
* Availability during U.S. business hours with real-time responsiveness throughout the shift 

You'll stand out if you have: 
* Shopify experience, including theme editing (Liquid, CSS, JavaScript), custom domain and DNS setup, app troubleshooting, and Shopify admin support 
* Experience with Rackspace Email and/or OpenSRS, including mailbox setup, client configuration, deliverability troubleshooting, and email account migration 
* Experience with GoDaddy for domain management, DNS configuration, and registrar-level settings 
* Experience using FreshDesk or a comparable help desk/ticketing platform 
* Experience with custom CMS platforms, WordPress, or other website management systems 
* Experience writing knowledge base articles, technical documentation, or internal troubleshooting guides 
* Experience working with U.S.-based clients or companies 
* Familiarity with Git or other version-control workflows 
* Relevant certifications such as CompTIA A+, Network+, Cloudflare Fundamentals, or Shopify Partner are helpful but not required 

BENEFITS 
* Competitive compensation with 13th month pay (USD or PHP) 
* Long-term engagement with a people-first U.S. agency 
* Consistent schedule: Monday through Friday, U.S. business hours 
* Remote/work-from-home flexibility 
* Real collaboration with a tight-knit U.S. team doing work that matters 

MORE ABOUT CYBERNAUTIC 

Cybernautic designs, develops, and manages the most thrilling and effective web experiences for businesses and organizations of all sizes. We’re a tight-knit team in Bloomington-Normal, IL. We believe hard work, creativity, and innovation move businesses forward — and that the web is one of the most powerful tools for making that happen. We’re investing in AI not to replace great work, but to amplify it. 

We’re looking for someone who shares that vision and wants to help shape what that looks like for our clients and our team. We know what we need and we’re passionate about finding the right fit. If this sounds like you, we’d love to meet you.

title: Bookkeeper / Monthly Financial Review
description:
Purpose of the role JobTracks operates a Manual & AI-assisted bookkeeping pipeline: source documents (PDF invoices, Excel payroll and AP reports, credit-card feeds) are extracted and classified into QuickBooks Online via manual entry and a structured import tool. This role should not need to perform manual data entry. Its purpose is to review, reconcile, correct, and close the books each month — to be the human control layer that catches what the data entry gets wrong and to own the accuracy of the financial record.

Core responsibilities Review transaction entries for correct account classification, flagging and correcting misclassifications before close. Reconcile all bank and credit-card accounts monthly against statements. Verify that automated invoice, bill, and expense imports match source documents on a sampled and exception basis. Confirm payroll entries tie out to ADP records (ADP retains responsibility for payroll tax filing). Manage the exception queue — any transaction the automation flags as low-confidence or cannot classify. Perform monthly close and produce P&L, balance sheet, and cash-flow statements. Identify control gaps or recurring error patterns in the automated pipeline and report them so the upstream rules can be improved.
What this role does NOT do Manual keying of invoices or expenses. Payroll tax filing (ADP). Tax preparation or strategic tax/decree advice (handled by outside CPA/tax counsel).

Required qualifications Hands-on QuickBooks Online proficiency, including reconciliation and month-end close. Demonstrated bookkeeping experience for a company with 1,000+ monthly transactions. Comfort reviewing and correcting machine-generated entries rather than originating them i.e., an editor's mindset, not a data-entry mindset. Strong attention to classification accuracy and an instinct for what "looks wrong."

Preferred Experience with multi-entity bookkeeping (JobTracks operates more than one company). Familiarity with QuickBooks import tools (SaasAnt, Transaction Pro, or similar).

Engagement terms Monthly retainer, scope sized to transaction volume. Specific monthly deliverable: reconciled accounts and closed books with financial statements delivered by [X] business days after month-end.

title: AU Mortgage Credit Analyst
description:
About EasyStaff

EasyStaff partners with Australian businesses to provide high-quality offshore team members across mortgage broking, legal, administration, and professional services industries.

We are currently seeking an experienced Mortgage Credit Analyst to support an Australian mortgage brokerage.

This is a senior-level role best suited to candidates with strong Australian mortgage lending experience and the ability to assess, structure, and present loan scenarios across multiple lenders.

Key Responsibilities

Credit Assessment & Scenario Analysis

- Analyse client financials including payslips, income structures, and employment types
- Accurately calculate base income, overtime, and variable earnings
- Interpret lender policies to determine borrowing capacity and loan suitability
- Run and compare multiple lender servicing calculators
- Use Quickli to assess borrowing capacity

Lender Policy & Niche Expertise (Critical)

- Develop a deep understanding of individual lender credit policies and niches
- Identify which lenders are best suited for each scenario based on their policy strengths and risk appetite
- Apply lender-specific rules such as:
      *Overtime treatment (100% vs shading)
      *Self-employed requirements (e.g. 1-year vs 2-year financials)
      *Income verification variations
- Understand and utilise lender niches such as:
      *ANZ – ability to consider 1-year self-employed financials in certain scenarios
      *Macquarie Bank – policy allowing no financials/accountant letters when company income is not being used
- Stay up to date with lender policy changes and proactively apply them to scenarios
- Use policy knowledge strategically to structure deals for approval, not just assessment

As part of your application, please include the names of three Australian lenders you have worked with or are most familiar with.

Self-Employed & Complex Income Analysis

- Analyse self-employed clients across:
      *Sole Traders
      *Partnerships
      *Companies
      *Trusts (including complex and multiple trust structures)
- Interpret financial documents including:
      *Tax Returns
      *Financial Statements
      *Notices of Assessment
- Accurately extrapolate income and normalise earnings
- Understand how to trace business income back to the individual applicant
- Identify add-backs, one-off expenses, and sustainability of income
- Apply lender-specific rules for self-employed income assessment

SMSF Lending (Desirable Knowledge)

- Understand the fundamentals of Self-Managed Super Fund (SMSF) lending
- Identify required documentation for SMSF loan applications including trust deeds, financial statements, and compliance documents
- Support occasional SMSF lending scenarios where required

Loan Structuring & Submission

- Prepare strong, well-structured loan submissions to maximise approval chances
- Draft detailed submission notes tailored to each lender
- Build and present lender scenarios to BDMs for policy confirmation
- Package deals in line with lender expectations and credit appetite

title: Executive Assistant
description:

Executive Assistant
About iSono Health Inc.

iSono Health is a dynamic and rapidly growing early clinical-commercial stage medical device company dedicated to saving lives and transforming care through innovative robotics, AI, and 3D ultrasound technologies. 

At iSono Health, we're pioneering a new era of breast care. We believe every woman deserves access to fast, affordable, and comfortable screenings. That's why we developed ATUSA, the world's first AI-powered platform with a point-of-care, automated 3D ultrasound system.

To succeed here, you need to think like an entrepreneur, act like a leader, and execute with precision. We value individuals who take initiative, embrace challenges, and are excited to wear multiple hats. Creativity, resilience, and a commitment to excellence are essential as we push the boundaries of what’s possible in healthcare.

Our work is exciting because it’s meaningful. Together, we’re empowering women to take control of their health by expanding access to potentially lifesaving ultrasound technology. If you’re passionate about making a difference and ready to be part of a nimble, scrappy team that values impact over hierarchy, iSono Health is the place for you.

At iSono Health, success isn’t just about the work—it’s about the mindset. We’re a small but mighty team of dedicated individuals driven by a shared mission: revolutionizing women’s health and making a profound impact for patients and clinicians. With big ambitions and innovative plans, we thrive in a fast-paced, dynamic environment where every team member owns what they build.

Join us in transforming healthcare and be part of a team where every voice matters, every idea counts, and every action contributes to a greater purpose.

About the role: Remote in Pacific Timezone (PT)

The Executive Assistant will provide critical support to our CEO and executive team while also gaining hands-on experience in startup building, product launch and marketing initiatives. This role is perfect for an organized, highly energized, A-player, detail-oriented individual with a passion for health technology and a drive to make a difference in people’s lives and in an organization. You serve as a vital force multiplier for a team accelerating toward next financing round closure and global commercial expansion. Moving beyond mere calendar management, you proactively anticipate organizational needs, safeguarding the CEO's bandwidth as our most critical asset. From investor relations to clinical partnership logistics, you execute across functions with absolute poise and precision.

If you need to be told what to do next, this isn't your role. If you thrive on context-switching, operate with discretion, and take ownership of outcomes, read on.



EXECUTIVE ASSISTANT ASSIGNMENTS:

Provide comprehensive administrative support to the CEO and other executives.
Own CEO calendars, manages priorities, scheduling meetings, travel arrangements, and itineraries. Anticipate needs 2–3 steps ahead; resolve problems before they surface.
communication, including phone calls, emails, and correspondence.
Prepare presentations and reports for meetings and executive reviews.
Organize and maintain filing systems (both physical and digital).
Manage office logistics and ensure smooth day-to-day operations.
Possess excellent interpersonal and communication skills.
Demonstrate strong organizational and time management skills.
Maintain a high level of discretion and professionalism across personnel, legal, and financial matters
Prepare materials for meetings, board reviews, and executive presentations


MARKETING, INVESTORS, INTERNAL OPERATIONS AND OTHER ASSIGNMENTS:

Assist the team with developing and implementing marketing campaigns.
Conduct market research and competitor analysis.
Create social media content (posts, graphics, etc.) to promote iSono Health's technology.
Support with content creation for website and marketing materials.
Manage and analyze social media engagement metrics.
Assist with event planning and execution (trade shows, conferences).
Strong written and verbal communication skills.
Creative and resourceful with an eagerness to learn.
Proficient in social media platforms and basic graphic design tools (a plus).
Support active fundraising: track investor pipeline, coordinate follow-ups, manage document flow.
Manage data room access, NDA execution, and due diligence document requests.
Coordinate scheduling and logistics for investor meetings, partner calls, and clinical site visits.
WHAT WE'RE LOOKING FOR
 3+ years supporting a C-suite executive, startup or high-growth environment strongly preferred.
Unnervingly organized. You live by systems, not good intentions.
Exceptional written communication — you can refine an investor email or draft a LinkedIn post without hand-holding.
Strong confidentiality, discretion and high integrity.
Comfortable in the room when it matters: investor calls, partner meetings, board prep.
Proficient with Google Workspace, Microsoft, LinkedIn, Canva or equivalent tools.
Startup-ready: you don't wait for the process you build it.
Discreet, grounded, and unflappable under pressure.
Genuine interest in women's health, healthcare technology, or mission-driven work.
Proactive and positive attitude to work independently and initiate and complete projects.
Medical device or life sciences experience is a meaningful plus.
Strong project management skills is required.


WHO WE ARE

iSono Health is building the future of breast care. Our platform, ATUSA,  is the world's first wearable, AI-powered, automated 3D breast ultrasound system. FDA-cleared, clinically deployed across 14+ sites, and designed to make high-quality breast imaging accessible to every woman, everywhere.



We're a small, high-conviction team. We move fast, make decisions with data, and hold ourselves to a high standard, because the stakes are real. Breast cancer affects 1 in 8 women. Early detection saves lives. That's not a tagline. That's the work.



We're at an inflection point: Series A in process, clinical traction growing, and a product roadmap that extends well beyond one generation. The person in this role will have a front-row seat, and real responsibility, in what comes next.

title: FP&A Analyst
description:
We are seeking a highly analytical and detail-oriented FP&A Analyst to support finance operations within a fast-paced, data-driven, and high-performance environment.

The ideal candidate has hands-on experience in FP&A functions including budgeting, forecasting, financial analysis, management reporting, and variance analysis, combined with exposure to General Ledger (GL) accounting processes and strong technical/data skills such as SQL and reporting tools.

This role is ideal for finance professionals who are comfortable working cross-functionally, supporting leadership teams, analyzing large datasets, and translating financial data into actionable business insights.

Who This Role Is For

We are looking for professionals who thrive in:
• Fast-paced finance and shared services environments
• Data-driven and analytical organizations
• Cross-functional business teams
• Strategic finance and operational finance roles
• High-growth and process-oriented environments
• Roles requiring ownership, accountability, and problem-solving

Role Overview

The FP&A Analyst will support key finance and analytical functions including:
• Budgeting and forecasting
• Financial modeling and analysis
• Variance and trend analysis
• KPI and business performance reporting
• GL analysis and topside journal support
• Financial planning and operational finance support
• Executive and management reporting
• Data extraction and reporting analysis

Depending on business needs, the role may also support multi-entity reporting structures, operational finance initiatives, systems/process improvement, and ad hoc strategic analysis.

Key Responsibilities

Financial Planning & Forecasting
• Assist in preparing budgets, forecasts, and financial plans
• Support monthly, quarterly, and annual forecasting cycles
• Analyze actuals versus budget and forecast results
• Assist in operational, headcount, and expense planning
• Support scenario planning and forecasting model improvements

Financial Analysis & GL Support
• Perform variance, trend, profitability, and financial performance analysis
• Support GL analysis including topside entries, accrual review, and reconciliations
• Assist in month-end reporting and financial review processes
• Prepare executive-ready financial reports, dashboards, and management presentations
• Support KPI tracking and business performance reporting

Data Analytics & Reporting
• Utilize SQL for data extraction, manipulation, and reporting analysis
• Analyze large datasets and provide actionable insights
• Assist in building and improving dashboards and reporting tools
• Support finance reporting automation and process improvements
• Work with ERP and reporting systems to ensure data accuracy and reporting efficiency

Business Partnership & Cross-Functional Support
• Partner closely with Accounting, Operations, HR, and leadership teams
• Support strategic decision-making through financial analysis and reporting
• Assist business units with budgeting and reporting requirements
• Support ad hoc analysis, projects, and process improvement initiatives

Required Qualifications
• Bachelor’s Degree in Finance, Accounting, Economics, or related field
• Minimum 3+ years of FP&A, financial analysis, corporate finance, or related finance experience
• Strong experience in budgeting, forecasting, and variance analysis
• Exposure to GL accounting processes, reconciliations, accruals, or month-end close
• Hands-on SQL experience for data extraction and reporting analysis
• Advanced Microsoft Excel skills
• Experience with ERP systems, finance systems, and reporting tools
• Strong analytical, problem-solving, and financial modeling abilities
• Excellent attention to detail and organizational skills
• Strong written and verbal communication skills
• Ability to work independently and manage deadlines effectively

Preferred Qualifications
• Experience supporting US-based companies or offshore/shared services environments
• Experience in SaaS, technology, BPO, or high-growth environments
• Exposure to multi-entity finance operations
• Experience with Power BI, Tableau, Power Query, VBA, or similar reporting/analytics tools
• Experience with Workday Adaptive, Hyperion, SAP BPC, or similar FP&A systems is highly preferred
• CPA, CFA, MBA, or equivalent certifications are an advantage
• Experience working cross-functionally with leadership teams is preferred

Application Instructions

To be considered for this position, please submit the following:
• Your application letter
• Unrestricted link to your updated resume/CV

Subject Line:
FPAA – YOUR FULL NAME

Only applicants who meet the required qualifications will be contacted.

title: US B2B Cold Caller | $5/hr Base + Bonuses + Commission | Track to Manager | 3 Openings
description:
Are you a confident communicator who wants a REAL career path, not just another calling job?

MSET LLC is a Phoenix-based marketing agency. We run paid ads, lead capture, and CRM automation for US service-based businesses (landscaping, glass repair, cleaning, moving). Our average client retainer is $1,000+/month with 12+ month retention. This is HIGH-TICKET B2B sales. Your bookings drive real revenue, not vanity meetings.

We are scaling our cold calling team and hiring 3 callers right now. The first one to consistently hit numbers AND coach others moves to the Appointment Setting Manager track within 6 months ($3,500+/month).


WHAT YOU WILL DO

- Make 80 dials/day to US contractors using our GoHighLevel + Twilio phone system (we provide the US number)
- Run BANT-qualified conversations using our proven outlines (not rigid scripts) to book qualified discovery calls
- Aggressively follow up every interested lead. Most B2B contractors need 3 to 7 touches before booking
- Keep meticulous records in GoHighLevel: call outcomes, callbacks scheduled, pipeline stages updated in real time
- Daily 15-minute team standup, weekly 1:1 with the founder
- Hit 2 qualified bookings per day after the 3-week ramp


COMPENSATION (paid weekly via Wise in USD)

After you pass the paid trial week:

- Base wage: $5/hour x 40 hrs/week = $800/month
- Show bonus: $15 per qualified appointment that actually attends
- Close commission: 10% of first-month retainer (~$100/close average)

REALISTIC MONTHLY EARNINGS:
- Solid tier (3 closes, 12 qualified shows): $1,280/month (~Php 74,200)
- Top tier (5 closes, 17 qualified shows): $1,555/month (~Php 90,200)
- Star tier (8 closes, 25 qualified shows): $1,975/month (~Php 114,500)

Earnings are UNCAPPED. Top performers move to Senior Caller (Month 4) and then Manager track (Month 6+) earning $3,500+/month (~Php 203,000+).


PAID TRIAL WEEK

- 5 working days (Monday to Friday) at $3/hr = $120 total
- Pass criteria: book 3+ qualified appointments by EOD Friday
- If you pass: we move you to the full role ($5/hr base, a 67% pay bump, plus bonuses + commission unlocked)
- If you fall short: we part ways with full trial pay and a clean handshake

Trial pay is intentionally lower than the full role so the only people who push hard during the trial are the ones who actually want the long-term role.


WHO WE ARE LOOKING FOR (in priority order)

1. NATIVE-LEVEL AMERICAN ENGLISH ACCENT. Contractors must not be able to tell you are calling from overseas in the first 5 seconds. A voice sample is required with your application.

2. COACHABLE. We train you on everything: our outlines, GoHighLevel, the BANT framework, objection handling, and the US contractor world. Your ability to take feedback and improve weekly matters more than your prior experience.

3. CONFIDENT. On a cold call, tone wins the first 5 seconds. You sound like a peer professional, not a discount telemarketer.

4. HIGH PAIN TOLERANCE. Cold calling is brutal. You absorb 70+ rejections per day without losing  ---------- ntum.

5. DISCIPLINED + ORGANIZED. You hit dial volume daily without being micromanaged AND you keep ruthless records. Every callback scheduled, every "almost" lead followed up, nothing falls through the cracks.

BONUS POINTS (not required):
- Any sales experience: retail, real estate, insurance, D2C, B2B SDR, door-to-door
- US BPO experience (Concentrix, Teleperformance, Sutherland, Alorica, TaskUs)
- Lead scraping experience (Google Maps, Facebook, LinkedIn, Apollo)
- GoHighLevel familiarity
- BANT, MEDDIC, or CHAMP framework knowledge


WHAT YOU NEED

- Your own laptop (Mac or Windows, mid-range or better)
- Reliable home internet (10+ Mbps, wired preferred)
- Quiet workspace
- 18+ years old


WHAT WE PROVIDE

- Lead lists, ready to dial (we scrape + DNC-scrub before handing them over)
- US phone number via Twilio + all call and SMS costs covered
- GoHighLevel CRM access
- Cold call outlines + objection-handling playbook
- USB headset (we ship or reimburse)
- Direct training, weekly coaching, and direct founder access


HOURS

Full-time, 40 hours/week, Monday to Friday.

Best US calling windows: 7-9am, 11am-1pm, and 4-6pm local US time across Eastern, Central, Mountain, and Pacific zones. For Philippines callers, this maps to roughly 4pm to 8am Manila depending on which US zones you focus on. We will plan your exact window with you during onboarding.

Time tracking: we use Time Doctor on your machine starting Day 1 (standard for US-facing remote call teams). You are paid for tracked active time. Idle time pauses tracking automatically. This protects both of us and ensures fair, transparent pay.

Start date: ASAP.


HOW TO APPLY (all 4 items required)

Send your application to  ----------  with the subject line "Cold Caller Application - [Your Full Name]".

Include ALL four of the following or your application will not be reviewed:

1. 60-SECOND VOICE RECORDING of you pitching anything (a product, your last role, why we should hire you). Upload as an unlisted YouTube link, Google Drive audio, or Loom video. THIS IS THE MOST IMPORTANT ITEM.

2. A MEASURABLE METRIC you have improved over time (sales numbers, scores, fitness, anything you tracked and got better at).

3. YOUR WEEKLY PAY EXPECTATION in USD.

4. SCREENSHOT of your home internet speed test from speedtest.net.

NO BPO EXPERIENCE? If you have the voice and the grit but no formal BPO background, write "NO BPO BUT GRIT" at the top of your application. We will prioritize your review.


INTERVIEW PROCESS

1. Initial screen (24 to 48 hours): we review your voice sample and answers.
2. Live video interview (30 minutes via Google Meet) with the founder and tea ---------- mber.
3. Paid trial week.
4. Ongoing role: sign the IC Agreement and join the team.

First 30 complete applications will be reviewed within 48 hours. Apply now.

ABOUT THE ROLE

You will handle customer email tickets for our Shopify-based ecommerce dropshipping brand. That means working through an inbox of 80 to 100 tickets per shift in Gorgias: order status inquiries, cancellation requests, returns and refunds, damaged or lost package cases, and size or quality complaints. You will use our documented SOPs and reply macros to resolve cases consistently, professionally, and with care.

This is not a chat role, not a phone role, and not a social media role. It is email ticket handling in Gorgias, every day.

-------------------------

WHAT YOU WILL DO

- Reply to customer email tickets in Gorgias using our documented SOPs and reply macros
- Hit team KPIs on First Response Time (FRT) and resolution time
- Coordinate with our Supply Chain and Disputes teams when cases require handoff
- Process refunds, replacements, and partial refunds within the approved decision tree
- Escalate cases that fall outside policy to the CS Manager with a clear summary
- Flag products with recurring complaints to the Quality team on a weekly basis
- Keep clean notes on tickets so handoffs are seamless
-------------------------

WHAT YOU WILL HAVE FROM DAY 1

- A full Customer Support SOP covering every scenario you will encounter
- Gorgias reply macros for common situations (cancellations, RTS, damaged items, lost in transit, and more)
- A Risky Products SOP for weekly product quality reporting
- Direct access to the CS Manager for escalations and questions
- An existing CS team to learn from, not a brand new setup
You do not need to invent the playbook. We have built it. You need to execute it cleanly and consistently.

-------------------------

REQUIREMENTS

- Hands-on experience in ecommerce customer support, dropshipping customer support, OR Shopify-based CS (any combination works, dropshipping experience is a strong plus)
- Experience with Gorgias OR a comparable ticketing tool (Zendesk, Reamaze, Freshdesk). Direct Gorgias is preferred but we can train on it quickly if you have strong Zendesk experience.
- Comfort handling 80 to 100 tickets per shift without falling behind
- Strong written English: clear, empathetic, and professional
- Reliable internet and your own computer
Bonus (not required):
- Prior experience handling RTS (return-to-sender) situations
- Familiarity with chargebacks and dispute processes in dropshipping
- Prior experience with EU customers and their return expectations
-------------------------

SCHEDULE - READ THIS BEFORE APPLYING

This role is 7 days a week, every week. Our customers email every day including weekends and public holidays. If you cannot commit to working 7 days a week, please do not apply. We will not make exceptions, and applying anyway only wastes both our time.

You can flex your specific working hours within the day as long as tickets are handled within our FRT target.

-------------------------

COMPENSATION DETAILS

Paid weekly via Wise. Rate grows with performance and tenure, we promote from within for team leads and specialist roles.

-------------------------

HOW TO APPLY

Read this section carefully. Applications that do not follow these instructions will be rejected without review.

1. Put the word BAMBU in your subject line. Nothing else is required in the subject.
2. In your application message, answer these 4 questions in order. Answer each inline, no attachments, no Loom videos at this stage.
Q1. How long have you worked in ecommerce customer support, what kind of responsibilities did you have, and for what type of companies (dropshipping, DTC, marketplace, etc.)? Name the actual companies and your role there in detail.

Q2. Describe one difficult customer ticket you resolved. What was the issue, what did the customer want, and how did you handle it? Keep it specific.

Q3. What ticketing tools have you used (Gorgias, Zendesk, Reamaze, Freshdesk, etc.)? What ticket volume per shift have you typically handled, and what is the most you have ever handled in a shift?

Q4. Confirm your availability: hours per day, days per week (we need 7), and your working hours in your local timezone. Also confirm: are you currently employed, and if yes, what would your start date look like?

-------------------------

We read every application that follows the instructions. We are looking for one person for the long term, not a roster of temporary help.

title: General Accountant
description:
An Accounting Generalist handles a company's day-to-day financial operations. They are responsible for processing payroll, managing accounts payable and receivable, reconciling bank statements, and maintaining the general ledger, to say the least. The role ensures all financial transactions are accurately recorded and compliant with local tax laws and regulations.

Core Responsibilities
• Accounts Payable & Receivable: Process vendor bills, issue payments, monitor collections to maintain healthy cash flow.
• Reconciliation: Perform daily and monthly reconciliations of bank statements, credit cards, and petty cash accounts to ensure zero discrepancies.
• Payroll: Handle end-to-end payroll processing for employees, ensuring accuracy and compliance with local labor laws and tax regulations, Monitor employee lates, absences, holidays and deductions.
• Taxation: Prepare and assist in filing tax returns including withholding taxes, VAT, and income tax and assist in maintaining BIR Tax compliance and related documentation.
• QuickBooks Management: Maintain and manage company’s general ledger in QuickBooks or QuickBooks Online, organized records through chart of accounts, classes, and locations.
• Financial Reporting: Prepare accurate and timely weekly report, monthly financial statements, and annual financial reports, generate profit and loss, balance sheets, and cash flow reports.
• Liquidation & Audits: Reconcile cash advances and liquidation reports, monitor expense liquidation and supporting documents, and prepare accounting records for internal or external audits.
• Commission & Purchasing: Calculate sales commissions accurately, match vendor invoices with purchase orders, and verify pricing agreements.
• Create and improve accounting processes and interna controls
• Develop Standard Operating Procedure (SOPs) for accounting-related concerns
• Establish process flow for all cash requests and financial approvals
• Maintain organized accounting records and documentation
• Perform such other functions authorized by the Management.

Qualifications:
• Education: Bachelor’s degree in Accounting, Finance, or a related field (or equivalent proven experience).
• Software Proficiency: Advanced, certified proficiency in QuickBooks or any related software and strong working knowledge of Microsoft Excel.
• Math Skills: Strong capability to apply financial formulas, specifically calculating percentages and commissions 
• Experience: 1-3 years of hands-on, full-cycle bookkeeping or accounting experience.

Skills:
• Goal-oriented, self-motivated, and proactive.
• Strong problem-solving and consultative skills.
• Ability to work independently and collaboratively.
• Exceptional attention to detail: high accuracy in data entry, auditing, and detecting financial discrepancies.
• Strong organizational, communication and presentation skills
• Ability to handle sensitive financial information with strict confidentiality.

title: ReadyMode Dialer Setup Specialist (One-Time Project)
description:

**Timeline:** Complete within 3-5 days

We need an experienced ReadyMode specialist to set up the dialer properly so the VA can start making outbound calls immediately.

1. Create 6 separate outbound campaigns:
   - Tax Delinquent
   - Open Liens
   - Fair/Poor Condition
   - Vacant Properties
   - Long-Term Owners
   - General Distressed

2. Set up 12 call dispositions:
   - No Answer, Voicemail Left, Wrong Number, Do Not Call, Not Interested, Follow Up, Call Back Later, Warm Lead, Hot Lead, Appointment Set, Offer Requested, Dead Lead

3. Configure recycling rules:
   - No Answer ? auto redial
   - Voicemail Left ? auto redial
   - Follow Up ? scheduled callback
   - Do Not Call ? permanently remove
   - Wrong Number ? permanently remove

4. Configure local presence caller IDs for Washington State

5. Upload and map lead list CSV to the correct campaigns (we provide the files)

6. Assign agent to campaign playlists

7. Configure dialer speed settings

8. Test the full flow -- dial a test lead, submit a call result, confirm it syncs to GHL automatically

9. Brief the client on daily dialer operation

**What we provide:**
- Full ReadyMode admin access
- Lead list CSV files ready to upload
- GHL integration already live and tested

Lead Generation & Business Development Specialist

Type: Full-Time · Remote
Experience: 3–5 Years
Industry: Commercial Construction / Building Materials

Web Scraping   ·   Email Campaigns   ·   Cold Outreach   ·   CRM Management   ·   Pipeline Reporting

ROLE OVERVIEW

US Panels manufactures insulated metal panel (IMP) systems used in cold storage, warehouses, food processing plants, data centers, and industrial buildings. Our customers are the contractors, developers, engineers, and facility owners who design and build these projects across North America.
The Lead Generation & Business Development Specialist is the engine behind our outbound pipeline. This is a high-activity, data-driven role built for someone who is equal parts researcher, communicator, and organizer. You will identify new industry verticals, build targeted prospect lists through web scraping and research, execute multi-channel outbound campaigns, and ensure every qualified lead is captured and tracked in the CRM. You work closely with the content team and sales team to keep messaging sharp and pipeline moving.

KEY RESPONSIBILITIES

Web Scraping & List Building
• Identify and research new industry verticals and geographic markets for outbound prospecting (cold storage developers, food processing contractors, industrial GCs, data center builders, warehouse owners, etc.).
• Use web scraping tools and research platforms to compile accurate, targeted contact lists — names, titles, company, email, phone, and LinkedIn.
• Leverage tools such as LinkedIn Sales Navigator, Apollo, ZoomInfo, Hunter.io, or similar platforms to source and verify contact data.
• Continuously expand and refresh prospect databases as new verticals are identified or existing lists age out.
• Segment lists by industry vertical, company size, geography, and decision-maker role to enable precise targeting.

Email Campaign Execution
• Build and launch outbound email sequences targeting segmented prospect lists — cold outreach, follow-ups, and re-engagement campaigns.
• Write or customize campaign copy in coordination with the content team to ensure messaging is relevant, personable, and aligned with current marketing initiatives.
• A/B test subject lines, email copy, and calls to action to continuously improve open rates and reply rates.
• Monitor deliverability, bounce rates, unsubscribes, and replies; maintain list hygiene and CAN-SPAM compliance.
• Coordinate send timing, frequency, and segmentation to avoid audience fatigue and maximize response.
B2B Outreach — Cold Email, Cold Call & LinkedIn
• Execute high-volume, personalized outbound outreach across three channels: email, phone, and LinkedIn.
• Make introductory calls to email-engaged prospects to qualify interest and move them toward a sales conversation.
• Send personalized LinkedIn connection requests and follow-up messages to targeted contacts at priority accounts.
• Maintain a consistent daily outreach cadence and log all activity in the CRM without exception.
• Represent US Panels professionally and build genuine rapport — you are the first human impression for most prospects.
Lead Capture & CRM Entry
• Enter all new contacts, outreach activity, responses, and lead status updates into the CRM accurately and promptly.
• Qualify inbound leads from the website and digital campaigns before passing to the sales team.
• Document lead context thoroughly at the time of handoff: company background, project type, timeline, decision-maker, and any expressed needs.
• Ensure zero leads fall through the cracks — own follow-up cadences until a lead is disqualified or handed off.
Cross-Team Collaboration
• Work closely with the content team to align outbound messaging with active campaigns, blog content, and downloadable assets.
• Coordinate with the sales team on target account priorities, territory coverage, and feedback on lead quality.
• Share insights on which verticals, messages, and contact types are responding so both teams can adapt quickly.
• Participate in regular pipeline and campaign review meetings.
Reporting & Analytics
• Report weekly on outreach volume, open rates, reply rates, calls made, meetings booked, and leads entered into the CRM.
• Track pipeline value generated from BDR-sourced leads and report on conversion at each stage.
• Identify what is working across channels, verticals, and messaging — and what is not — and bring recommendations to the team.
• Maintain a clean, organized view of campaign performance in the CRM and reporting dashboards.

REQUIRED SKILLS & EXPERIENCE

Lead Generation & Sales
• 3–5 years of experience in a BDR, SDR, lead generation, or outbound sales role.
• Proven track record building prospect lists from scratch and converting cold outreach into qualified pipeline.
• Experience selling into or prospecting construction, building materials, manufacturing, or industrial B2B markets is strongly preferred.
Data Scraping & Research
• Hands-on experience using web scraping tools, data enrichment platforms, or research workflows to build contact lists.
• Proficiency with Apollo, ZoomInfo, LinkedIn Sales Navigator, Hunter.io, or comparable prospecting tools.
• Ability to identify the right contacts within target companies and verify data accuracy before outreach.

Email Marketing & Outreach
• Experience writing and executing outbound email campaigns using HubSpot, Mailchimp, Apollo, Outreach, or similar platforms.
• Comfortable crafting personalized, concise outreach that does not read like a mass email.
• Familiarity with email deliverability, list hygiene, and CAN-SPAM compliance basics.
CRM & Data Entry
• Proficient in HubSpot, Pipedrive, Salesforce, or a comparable CRM platform.
• Meticulous about data accuracy — contact records, activity logs, and pipeline stages are always current and correct.
• Highly organized; able to manage large lists, multi-touch cadences, and concurrent campaigns without losing track.
Communication & Collaboration
• Strong written communication — outreach emails are clear, personable, and compelling.
• Comfortable on the phone; able to build rapport quickly with busy construction professionals.
• Collaborative team player who communicates proactively with content and sales counterparts.

PREFERRED QUALIFICATIONS

• Experience prospecting into cold storage, food processing, data center, or warehouse construction markets.
• Familiarity with commercial construction project timelines and procurement decision-making.
• Exposure to trade shows or industry associations (AGC, GCCA, IARW, BOMA).
• Experience with LinkedIn outreach automation tools (Lemlist, Dripify, or similar).

TOOLS USED IN THIS ROLE

Apollo / ZoomInfo / Hunter.io   ·   LinkedIn Sales Navigator   ·   HubSpot or Pipedrive CRM

Outreach / Lemlist or similar   ·   Mailchimp / HubSpot Email   ·   Google Sheets / Workspace

PERFORMANCE METRICS

• Qualified prospects added to CRM per week and month.
• Outbound email open rates, reply rates, and positive response rates.
• Cold calls made and conversion to booked conversations.
• Meetings booked and handed off to the sales team.
• Pipeline value generated from outbound-sourced leads.

HOW TO APPLY

Please submit the following:
1. Resume or CV
2. Brief description of the industries you have prospected into and the tools you used.
3. An example of an outbound email or campaign sequence you wrote (anonymized if needed).

Applicants with demonstrated success building pipeline from cold outreach in technical or industrial B2B markets will be strongly preferred.
