title: Full-Stack Engineer (Node + React) - Long- Term, Full-Time, Real Estate Tech Startup
description:
ABOUT US
We are building a new kind of real estate platform in the United States. It is called The Buyer Listing Service.
Today, real estate websites only show homes that are for sale. We are building the buy side of the market for real estate agents. On our platform, real estate agents post buyer want-listings, not for sale listings. Our system then uses AI to match both buyer agents and seller agents together. We work directly with the official real estate databases (called MLSs) that agents use across the US. More of these databases are signing up with us every month and we're growing fast. 

We are a small and fast-moving team. When you write code here, it ships quickly and real people use it. This is a rare chance to help build a brand-new product from a growth stage, take real ownership, and grow with the company.

THE KIND OF PERSON WE'RE LOOKING FOR
We are looking for an experienced engineer who can work independently. Over time, you will own and run large parts of this platform. We are relying on your expertise. This is not a junior role and we are not looking for someone we train from the basics. If you need a senior developer checking all of your work, this is not the right fit. If you want real ownership and the chance to be the technical expert on a growing product, it is a great fit.

WHAT YOU WILL DO
• Build and improve features across our website and our backend API
• Review and merge code changes (we use a clean pull request workflow on GitHub)
• Connect and clean real estate data from different sources so it fits our system
• Use AI tools (like Claude) to help map and clean messy data
• Work closely with the founder and one other developer, and increasingly own your areas

YOU MUST HAVE
• 4+ years of professional full-stack experience, and the proven ability to work on your own
• Strong fundamentals, not just frameworks. You can explain how JavaScript works (for example, the event loop), how the web works, and how to model and query a SQL database.
• Strong skills in Node.js and Express
• Strong skills in React
• Comfortable writing raw SQL (we use MySQL, no ORM)
• Real experience integrating and cleaning messy data and APIs from other systems
• A habit of learning on your own. You have personal projects or things you built to teach yourself.
• Clean Git habits and small, focused pull requests
• Good written English (most of our work is async and in writing)
• Able to follow written rules and instructions exactly

HOW WE THINK ABOUT AI
We use AI tools like Claude every day and we want people who use them well. But we are hiring an experienced engineer, not a person who copies and pastes from a chatbot. You should be able to explain why your code works, and fix it when the AI gives a wrong answer. In our process we will always ask you to explain your decisions, so deep experience matters more than fast answers.

NICE TO HAVE
• Experience with AWS
• Experience using AI or LLMs (Claude, OpenAI, etc.) in real projects • Experience with data pipelines or ETL

HOW WE WORK
• We write small, focused changes. We do not refactor code that is not part of the task.
• We follow a written set of rules for the codebase. Following instructions matters more here than working fast.
• We welcome AI tools. We want people who use them well to do more.

HOURS
Full-time, 40 hours per week. We would like you to work US East Coast hours if you can. This helps you work at the same time as the rest of the team.

HOW TO APPLY
1. Start your message with the word BLUEPRINT so we know you read this post.
2. Send a link to one project you built and are proud of. Tell us in 2 or 3 sentences what it does, what part you built, and why you built it that way. Be ready to explain your choices on a call.
3. Tell us your experience with Node, React, and SQL, and the largest project you have owned or led.
4. Tell us if you have used AI or LLM tools in your work, and how.
Our process is a short call, then a deeper technical interview, then a small paid task using fake data, then a short call to walk through your code together. We will reply to strong applicants.

title: Engineer  - Adobe - AI Retouching & Workflow Engineer (Portrait / Fashion-Level Studio)
description:
Job Overview
We are a NYC-based premium portrait studio specializing in maternity, newborn, and beauty work. We are rebuilding our entire retouching pipeline using AI tools and are looking for a technically strong, detail-obsessed teammate to join us long-term.

This role combines:

High-end portrait retouching (Vogue-level quality)

AI-assisted workflow design and optimization ( ---------- , Imagen, Photoshop, Lightroom)

Process documentation so the system can be used and taught to others

If you are an engineer / technical creative who loves problem-solving, software, and visual quality, this is for you.

Responsibilities
Retouching & Visual Quality

Retouch high-resolution RAW images for maternity, newborn, and beauty portraiture

Perform advanced skin work (dodge & burn, texture preservation, natural results – no plastic skin)

Refine tones, contrast, and color grading suitable for large-format fine art prints

Clean details: hair, background, wardrobe, and small distractions

Maintain consistency of look across full sets and across multiple shoots

Learn and match a Vogue-level editorial standard

Reference quality: Lola Melanie, Annie Leibovitz

AI & Workflow / Engineering

Help design and refine an AI-assisted retouching pipeline using:

 ----------  plugins

Imagen AI

Photoshop (latest)

Lightroom (latest)

Map the current process from RAW to final print and identify bottlenecks

Create and refine Photoshop actions, batch processes, and smart presets to standardize the flow

Experiment with and evaluate new tools/features for speed and consistency

Record your screen and document your steps to build internal training material and SOPs

Proactively propose improvements to speed, quality, and reliability

Ideal Background
You do not have to be a traditional career retoucher, but you must bring a strong mix of technical ability + visual sensitivity.

You might be a great fit if you:

Have a background in engineering, computer science, 3D, or other technical fields

Are very comfortable learning new software, figuring things out from tutorials and documentation

Have solid Photoshop and Lightroom experience (retouching, layers, masks, color work)

Enjoy building and optimizing systems, not just “doing tasks”

Care deeply about detail and visual quality, even if you’re still growing toward top fashion-level retouching

Bonus points if you:

Have experience with 3D modeling, CAD, or technical graphics

Have used AI or automation tools (plugins, scripts, actions, or no-code workflow tools)

Have any portfolio in photography, retouching, 3D, or design

Have worked on beauty, fashion, or portrait images before

Requirements
Strong technical mindset and problem-solving skills

Experience with Photoshop and Lightroom (or strong evidence you can ramp up very quickly)

Willingness to practice and accept critique to reach a Vogue-level, natural, refined look

Stable internet and a reliable computer capable of handling high-resolution files

Ability to work graveyard shift (first month), then flexible schedule with some overlap

Good written English for communication and process documentation

Looking for stable, long-term remote work (Philippines-based preferred)

Trial Task
Shortlisted candidates will be asked to complete a small paid test, which includes:

Retouching a small set of images (around 3–5) using our tools

Providing a screen recording of your retouching process with light explanation:

How you structure your workflow and layers

How and where you use AI vs manual work

Any ideas you see for improving speed and consistency

This test helps us evaluate both your eye for quality and your engineering mindset.

How to Apply

Cover letter

Resume

Portfolio (before/after examples strongly preferred)

Links to any social profiles and website/portfolio

title: Creative Internship: Learn and Earn
description:
We Don't Just Hire VAs. We Build Them.
Elorae works with companies such as Leaders Academy, a speech and debate coaching company based in Seoul, Korea. We work with clients across Asia and we run fast. Our team is small, our standards are high, and everything we produce is seen by real students, real parents, and real partners.
We are opening internship slots for college students who want to do real work, build a real portfolio, and get paid while doing it.
This is not a coffee-fetching internship. You will be assigned to an active workstream from week one.

How It Works
You start at 5 hours in week one. By month three, you can be at 20 hours. The pace depends on you. Every intern earns 100 PHP per hour from day one. No trial period without pay. No unpaid probation.
If your school requires official documentation for academic credit, we coordinate directly with your institution.
If you perform, you are first in line when a paid VA position opens. We hire from within.

What We Are Looking For
We organize our internship positions into three tiers.
???? Green — Actively recruiting each term:
Graphic Designer — You work in Canva daily and have a portfolio that shows it. You design social media graphics, event banners, marketing assets, and YouTube thumbnails for real programs with real audiences. If your work is strong, it goes live immediately.
Copyeditor — You catch what others miss. You edit student essays, program materials, and organizational communications with precision and consistency. Chicago Manual of Style is not a stranger to you. Strong English is non-negotiable.
Social Media Manager — You understand platforms, timing, and what makes content land. You manage scheduling, captions, and posting across channels. You are not just a user of social media. You think about it strategically.
Media Editor — You edit video with real software, not just phone apps. You produce polished short-form content, event highlights, and branded video assets. A portfolio with actual edited footage is required.

???? Yellow — Limited placement for exceptional candidates, irregular availability:
Web Developer — You have built or significantly maintained a real website and can show it.
Personal Assistant — You are exceptionally organized, proactive, and comfortable supporting a fast-moving executive directly.
Events Coordinator — You have coordinated a real event from planning through execution and can walk us through what you did.
Tutor — You have subject mastery in English, writing, or speech and debate and genuine teaching instinct.

???? Red — VA services exist in these roles. We do not host interns here:
Accounts Technician
Data Analyst
Administrative Assistant
Software Developer

What You Need
You are currently enrolled in a college or university in the Philippines. You are at least a second-year student. You have a stable internet connection: minimum 30 Mbps download, 5 Mbps upload, under 50 ms ping. You have a dedicated workspace and a working laptop or desktop. Sessions and check-ins are live on camera. A bad connection wastes everyone's time.

What You Get
This is paid work. You earn 100 PHP per hour from day one. You build a real portfolio on real programs. You receive a certificate of completion from Leaders Academy. If your institution requires official internship documentation for academic credit, we coordinate directly with your school. And if you perform, you are first in line when a VA position opens. We hire from within.

How to Apply
Complete our application form:  ---------- 
In the form, indicate which tier and role you are applying for. Green tier applicants must include a portfolio link.

Attentiveness Check
Write "Twenty" in your subject line. This tells us you read the full posting. Applications without it will not be reviewed.

One Last Thing
If you are looking for easy hours without doing real work, this is not the right fit. The internship structure is designed to show us what you can do and help you grow. We will know within the first two weeks.

title: Senior Amazon PPC Account Coordinator - Long-Term Career Role
description:
We're a boutique Amazon advertising agency serving US and European clients, and an Amazon Ads Verified Partner. We've built the agency around long client tenures and a small core team that has worked together for years.

About the Role:
You'll be the daily point of contact for our US clients. You'll work directly with the founder and the campaign team, owning client communication, weekly reporting, and account context. Your job is to make sure every client feels heard, informed, and well-served.

This is a long-term role. We're looking for someone who wants to build a career with one company, not someone looking for the next gig in 6 months. If that's you, keep reading.

What you'll do:

Monitor and respond to client messages on Slack and Microsoft Teams, with live coverage during the daily US overlap window and 8 to 10 hour response time outside of it
Use our SOPs and account context to respond to routine account questions independently
Coordinate with our internal campaign team to surface client requests and deliver answers
Prepare weekly performance summaries for clients. We have templates and a reporting framework
Maintain detailed account notes so the team always has full context
Escalate strategic decisions and complex issues directly to the founder

What you bring:

2 to 4 years working with US Amazon clients at an agency or in-house at an Amazon brand
You've independently owned client accounts, not just assisted on them
Excellent written English. Non-negotiable, since you'll be the voice clients see
Strong knowledge of Amazon Seller Central and Amazon Advertising console
You understand the difference between ACoS and TACoS and can explain it clearly to a client
Comfortable using Slack, Microsoft Teams, Google Workspace daily
Reliable home internet and a quiet workspace for occasional client calls
Long-term mindset. You want stability, growth, and a team that values you

What we offer:

$7 to $10/hr starting rate based on experience, with formal review at 90 days
Clear growth path: part-time to full-time, coordinator to senior coordinator to account manager
Direct working relationship with the founder, not buried under layers of management
Small team where your work is seen and your voice matters
Paid Philippine public holidays

How to apply:
Send us a Loom video (3 to 4 minutes max) where you answer these three questions in order:

Tell us about yourself and the kind of role you're looking for next? 
Imagine your client is a smart business owner who doesn't know Amazon Ads well. In about 60 seconds, explain the difference between ACoS and TACoS, and why both matter.
A client messages you on Slack saying "Our ACoS jumped this week, what's going on?". You haven't looked at the data yet. What's your first reply to them, and why?

Attach your resume along with the Loom link. Applications without a Loom will not be reviewed. We'll respond to every serious submission within 5 business days. Trial task will be paid.
